title: Chapter Eighteen: Recommendation
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Thanks to Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo's consideration, the two White-ranks were able to head straight to the sleeping quarters of the pavilion. It was said to be the discretion of the Blue Warrior, trusted by all in Desolate Sect, along with universally recognised martial prowess. At times like this, it was certainly different from the military.

"Now that we're in, I don't feel like going out again," Heon Won-chang said. "I'll have to visit Xiangyang's Best Tavern next time."

"You'll probably train for about two hours. Young Master Jeong is quite something." He no longer even made a tired face. Just the resigned expression of a man who had accepted he was sharing a barracks with a creature from another world.

"Rest well," Yeon-shin said, and smiled slightly.

Three days back, and the word had spread.

The Blood Master Sword. A branch destroyed. By a White-rank. By a boy.

The shift was in everything. Throughout his way to the small martial arts hall, he was greeted cheerfully by seniors. But there was a new quality to it -- fist salutes delivered with careful precision, eyes that lingered a beat too long, voices that lowered when they spoke his name. A few people flinched upon receiving his fist salute in return, having felt his changed energy after completing the mission.

"The prodigy has changed."

"Looking at his energy alone, it's almost unrecognizable. They say boys around that age grow well, but this is a completely different story."

"The phrase 'constant self-renewal' can only be applied to that friend here."

"It won't be easy for us anymore, will it?"

No one could argue with the Blue Warrior who blurted out the last words. They all fell silent simultaneously. For a fifteen-year-old boy to have even a slight chance against the Blue-rank masters of Desolate Sect was a wonder in itself.

Yeon-shin barely listened. His sense of time was different from theirs. If he wanted to live, he needed to be able to discuss the martial power of the Divine Sword Squad Leader just before turning twenty. From White to Blue, then through Black like Ma Jin, and even to the unseen Purple. Someone like Ma Jin, the Radiant Demon Wing Leader who encompassed seventeen levels of martial power, was at that level. There was no room for leisure.

Come to think of it, the year had changed. He was sixteen now. In the civilian world, it was around the age to hold a wedding ceremony and start a family. The Jeong family, unlike typical martial arts families, had customs closer to those of common people. A third-rate martial arts family and major landowner.

*I wonder what kind of person I would have married.*

A meaningless imagination. He immediately raised the Dynamic Technique to erase such idle thoughts.

He trained in the small martial arts hall each evening, avoiding the occupied rooms where qi flowed and entering an empty training room. The Radiant Demon Formation flowed through fists and feet, drawing trajectories for every situation -- close-range hundred strikes, kicks into stamps into straight punches. The air trembled at the wave of force deliberately raised.

He had truly felt Ma Jin's teachings in the fight against the Blood Master Sword. His body, perfected by the Dynamic Technique, was an incredibly strong weapon in itself. Close combat was different from sword techniques. It was a fighting style that directly utilised his unique strengths -- the Grappling Hand, the reversed blade, the crushing grip.

*I want to properly create a fist method.*

As time passed, it would become harder to use his youthful appearance like a divine sword. He could not always rely on catching enemies off guard. He needed to take out all the weapons he could use and sharpen them keenly.

*Is there anything I could draw inspiration from?*

A sky-blue light flickered in his eyes.

---

Above, at the very top of the Radiant Demon Wing pavilion, in the Leader's quarters, Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo sat with Ma Jin.

"What was the prodigy's character like when standing at the boundary between life and death?" Ma Jin asked. His voice was careful, deliberate. "How did he treat his comrades?"

"The boundary between life and death?" Cheong Myeong asked back, his face blank.

Baek Mi-ryeo remained silent. *Was there such a thing?*

Ma Jin's scarred face showed perplexity. "Didn't you say there was a Blood Master Sword? That means a branch-level force was operating in secret. Don't tell me you two handled it alone to protect the White-ranks. The records bound in the transmission document have spatial limitations. Explain in detail."

Come to think of it, the two Blue-rank masters had more than one mission. The instruction to observe the prodigy's character. Now it seemed that this was considered even more important than the original mission in Zhenping County. Baek Mi-ryeo knew. There was no other reason why Ma Jin was asking so pressingly. It would be difficult to do this if he was not considering the prodigy as the next generation Radiant Demon Wing Leader.

That was why it was even more regrettable. There was nothing to say.

"Without a chance to see anything," Baek Mi-ryeo said carefully, "he annihilated the Blood Flame Cult's branch. The prodigy. Alone. All I can say now is that despite the arduous journey, he never forgot his manners. He didn't seem to be interested in the outstanding martial prowess he currently possesses."

Ma Jin fell silent with a pensive look on his face. The shock of single-handedly destroying the branch, including the Blood Master Sword, was like that. A realm where even the likes of the Nine Sects, Eight Families, Thirteen Heavens, and the level of Desolate Sect could be called masters. It was hard to express it even with the word *outstanding*.

"Cheong Myeong."

The question carried weight. There was a famous story about the eyes of the Elf clan, said to see the essence of things. After the founding of the nation, the Great-Great Empress Dowager, who became the consort of King Taejo, had used those eyes to unmask traitors. The current emperor had single-handedly faced the Hundred and Eight Arhat Formation of Shaolin Temple to witness that power. It was why the final test for entering Desolate Sect was a private audience with the lord in the first place.

What Ma Jin wanted to confirm was the qualifications as a leader -- a martial power that went beyond mere strength.

"He's excellent for his age," Cheong Myeong said with a light smile. "His killing intent is quite strong, but he knows how to handle it properly. In terms of temperament, there's something more extraordinary about him."

"His killing intent is strong?"

"Isn't it obvious? That friend, throughout the journey, never mentioned anything about his background. Judging by his words and actions, it's clear that he's learned, but..."

"I think so too," Baek Mi-ryeo added. "Both new recruits seem to have severed ties with their families in some way."

Ma Jin tapped the table with his fingers. Then he slowly nodded.

"Before thinking about a successor, taking care of the ones who have become our family comes first. There's plenty of time. Like this time too. I'm curious to see how many more unexpected achievements he will make. It'll be fun to nurture him."

"I do wonder when he'll become a Blue-rank," Cheong Myeong said. "It doesn't feel too far off."

The conversation was closer to a chat than a report. The night sky slowly draped over the Radiant Demon Wing pavilion.

---

The next morning.

"Prodigy. The Grand Master is calling for you."

The White-rank senior's tone was cautious. Different from before. Whenever he was in the grand martial arts hall, he would hear words like "the Blood Master Sword..." whispered a few times. Even now, three days after his return.

Among the thirty-six members of Radiant Demon Wing, ten were White-ranks, so it was rare indeed for a White-rank to single-handedly discuss a branch of the Blood Flame Cult. There were even talks that he might become the youngest Blue-rank in the shortest time.

"Yes, I will go up," Yeon-shin said, clasping his hands politely.

Whenever he heard people highly evaluating him, he tried to maintain an even more upright demeanour. He did not think an absolute master without popularity could rise to the position of the Divine Sword Squad Leader. Perhaps thanks to that, the way people looked at him was improving day by day. To the point where he himself felt warmth.

*Is it a new mission?*

Although they guaranteed a month's rest for a warrior who had completed a mission, he did not fully believe those words. How could one predict what would happen in a martial arts sect?

He climbed to Ma Jin's quarters at the top of the pavilion. Ma Jin's thick voice rang out before he could knock.

"Come in."

*He sensed my presence right away. Even though I was training to fully embody the wind-and-grass technique. The Grand Master indeed.*

He opened the door and entered the room. And slightly flinched.

Ma Jin's face had become extremely serious. The scar across his face looked as if it had hardened permanently.

"Did something happen? An important mission?"

"Although you are showing unprecedented achievements, it's not yet to the extent where Radiant Demon Wing can entrust you with what could be considered an important mission. Of course, the expectations are high." The scar wriggled -- Ma Jin was trying to smile, showing pride in his own way. "It's not a mission. Well, it's not something that can be compared to an ordinary mission either. The lord has asked me to bring you along."

"The lord of Desolate Sect?"

The absolute ruler with green eyes came to mind. It was incomprehensible.

"Are there cases where a White-rank warrior who has finished an interview is summoned again?"

"There is no precedent, so I'm not at ease. I can't fathom her intentions at all." Ma Jin gestured with a grave expression. "Even so, we can't delay. Let's go."

He followed Ma Jin out of the Radiant Demon Wing pavilion. There was quite a distance to the main castle where the Lord of Desolate Sect resided. Even though Yeon-shin matched Ma Jin's pace and walked briskly, it took about the time to drink a cup of tea.

During that walk, all the attention was focused on them. Perhaps it was because of the noticeable scar on Ma Jin, wearing the black martial uniform of Desolate Sect, that caught people's eyes. Everyone recognised the Radiant Demon Wing Leader, who was said to create scenes of hell in every battlefield he passed through. At the same time, attention was also focused on the boy walking half a step behind.

"It's the prodigy of Radiant Demon Wing."

"The one who killed the Blood Master Sword?"

"He looks even younger than I thought."

It was said that the existence of a boy wearing a white martial uniform was unique to Jeong Yeon-shin in all of Desolate Sect. It was still unfamiliar to be recognised by strangers he had never met.

Yeon-shin maintained an indifferent expression and silently followed behind Ma Jin. Up the long staircase. Through the castle gates, where the guard warriors did not stop them -- whether due to the prestige of a Black-rank master or trust in the martial power of the lord herself. To the door of the Sect Leader's chamber.

The door opened on its own.

A cool breeze. The trunk of the great tree piercing through the chamber. And sitting on a branch of it, the Lord of Desolate Sect.

Deep green eyes passed over Ma Jin and fixed on Yeon-shin.

"Come here."

"We greet the lord."

He followed Ma Jin's fist salute. Raised his head.

Two others stood in the chamber.

An old man. Enormous build. Sharp eyes. A white beard without a single impurity, reaching to his solar plexus. Purple robes with a standing collar. The aura flowing from him was immense -- diminished only by the Sect Leader's presence.

*A purple-rank master. Above even the Grand Master.*

Beside the old man, a boy. Handsome. Arrogant jaw. He stared at Yeon-shin with eyes of stark black and white.

A challenge.

"So you're that Radiant Demon Wing prodigy," the old man said. His voice filled the room. "I am Ma Yeon-jeok, who once led the Divine Sword Squad."

*Ma.*

"My grandson wanted to see a master of his age. It's nice to meet you."

Yeon-shin let the words pass through him. His gaze went to the Sect Leader.

She raised one corner of her mouth.

Something entered his mind. Not a sound transmission. No flow of energy. A voice that simply appeared.

*I will officially recognize you, if you wish.*

He looked at his maternal grandfather.

---
